headline: The Starter Home Market Is Down 300,000 Homes From 2019
summary: The national starter home shortage that peaked in 2022 is beginning to ease, but a new Realtor.com® report finds the recovery is anything but even. Nationally, there are roughly 300,000 fewer homes priced under $350,000 on the market today than in June 2019, and the typical starter home now costs $344,000, up from $256,000 seven years ago.

headline: Trump Lawsuits Reach Key Stages After Pair of Recent Setbacks
